---
title: "Model"
output: html_document
---


```{r }
library(tidyverse)
library(plm)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(stargazer)
library(broom)
library(patchwork)


 d <- readRDS("health_analysis_cleaned.rds")
```

```{r }
# Data manipulation and attrition % check

d_analysis <- d %>%
  filter(syear < 2023) %>%  # Exclude 2023 (no attrition observable)
  mutate(
    # Standardize only BMI and grip (different units)
    bmi_std = scale(bmi)[,1],
    
    # Employment status categories
    employed = ifelse(pgemplst %in% c(1, 2, 3, 4), 1, 0) # 5 is unemployed, rest is occupied
  )

# Summary of attrition by year
cat("=== Attrition Summary by Year ===\n")
d_analysis %>%
  group_by(syear) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_attrition = sum(attrition == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_attrition = round(100 * n_attrition / n, 2)
  ) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
```

```{r }
# ==============================================================================
# 2. Model 1: Basic health measures
# ==============================================================================

model1 <- glm(attrition ~ mcs + pcs + bmi_std + age + female,
              data = d_analysis,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))

cat("\n=== Model 1: Basic Health Measures ===\n")
summary(model1)


# ==============================================================================
# 3. Model 2: Add SF-12 subscales
# ==============================================================================

model2 <- glm(attrition ~ pf_nbs + rp_nbs + bp_nbs + gh_nbs + 
                vt_nbs + sf_nbs + re_nbs + mh_nbs +
                bmi_std + age + female,
              data = d_analysis,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))

cat("\n=== Model 2: SF-12 Subscales ===\n")
summary(model2)


# ==============================================================================
# 4. Model 3: Add grip strength
# ==============================================================================

model3 <- glm(attrition ~ mcs + pcs + bmi_std + grip_max +
                age + female,
              data = d_analysis,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))

cat("\n=== Model 3: With Grip Strength ===\n")
summary(model3)


# ==============================================================================
# 5. Model 4: Full model with socioeconomic controls
# ==============================================================================

model4 <- glm(attrition ~ bmi_std + grip_max +
              pf_nbs + rp_nbs + bp_nbs + gh_nbs + vt_nbs + sf_nbs + re_nbs + mh_nbs +
              age + female + employed + migback + partner + 
              pgtatzeit + pgbilzeit + factor(pgmode) +
              factor(psample) + factor(syear),
              data = d_analysis,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))

cat("\n=== Model 4: Full Model with Controls ===\n")
summary(model4)


# ==============================================================================
# 5. Model 5: Full model with socioeconomic controls - Dummed-down health data
# ==============================================================================

model5 <- glm(attrition ~ bmi_std + grip_max +
              mcs + pcs +
              age + female + employed + migback + partner + 
              pgtatzeit + pgbilzeit + factor(pgmode) +
              factor(psample) + factor(syear),
              data = d_analysis,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))

cat("\n=== Model 5: Full Model dummed down health ===\n")
summary(model5)


```


# ==============================================================================
# Model 4 Bayesian: Full model with socioeconomic controls
# ==============================================================================

```{r }
library(rstanarm)
library(bayesplot)

# ==============================================================================
# Model 4 Bayesian: Full model with socioeconomic controls
# ==============================================================================

set.seed(42)  # For reproducibility

# Fit Bayesian logistic regression with weakly informative priors
model4_bayes <- stan_glm(
  attrition ~ bmi_std + grip_max +
    pf_nbs + rp_nbs + bp_nbs + gh_nbs + vt_nbs + sf_nbs + re_nbs + mh_nbs +
    age + female + employed + migback + partner + 
    pgtatzeit + pgbilzeit + factor(pgmode) +
    factor(psample) + factor(syear),
  data = d_analysis,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  
  # Priors
  prior = normal(0, 2.5),           # Weakly informative prior for coefficients
  prior_intercept = normal(0, 5),   # Prior for intercept
  
  # MCMC settings
  chains = 4,                        # Number of Markov chains
  iter = 2000,                       # Total iterations per chain
  warmup = 1000,                     # Burn-in iterations
  cores = 8,                         # Parallel processing
  refresh = 250,                     # Progress updates
  
  # Diagnostics
  seed = 42
)

cat("\n=== Model 4 Bayesian: Full Model with Controls ===\n")
print(model4_bayes, digits = 3)

# Model diagnostics
cat("\n=== Bayesian Diagnostics ===\n")
cat("R-hat values (should be < 1.01):\n")
rhat_vals <- summary(model4_bayes)[, "Rhat"]
print(rhat_vals)
cat("\nAny problematic R-hat values?:", any(rhat_vals > 1.01, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

cat("\nEffective sample sizes (should be > 400):\n")
neff_vals <- summary(model4_bayes)[, "n_eff"]
print(summary(neff_vals))
cat("Any low ESS?:", any(neff_vals < 400, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Posterior predictive checks
cat("\n=== Posterior Predictive Checks ===\n")

# Check mean attrition rate
pp_check(model4_bayes, plotfun = "stat", stat = "mean") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Mean Attrition Rate",
       subtitle = "Does the model reproduce the observed attrition rate?")

# Check binary outcome distribution
pp_check(model4_bayes, plotfun = "bars", nreps = 50) +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Binary Outcome Distribution",
       subtitle = "Observed (dark) vs Predicted (light) attrition")

# Compare with frequentist model
cat("\n=== Comparison: Frequentist vs Bayesian ===\n")
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(coef(model4)),
  Frequentist = coef(model4),
  Bayesian = coef(model4_bayes),
  Difference = coef(model4_bayes) - coef(model4)
)
print(comparison_df, digits = 3)

# Credible intervals (Bayesian) vs Confidence intervals (Frequentist)
cat("\n=== 95% Credible Intervals (Bayesian) ===\n")
bayes_ci <- posterior_interval(model4_bayes, prob = 0.95)
print(head(bayes_ci, 15))  # Show first 15 for readability

cat("\n=== 95% Confidence Intervals (Frequentist) ===\n")
freq_ci <- confint(model4)
print(head(freq_ci, 15))



# Model comparison metrics
cat("\n=== Model Fit Metrics ===\n")
cat("LOO (Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation):\n")
loo_model4 <- loo(model4_bayes)
print(loo_model4)

cat("\nWAIC (Watanabe-Akaike Information Criterion):\n")
waic_model4 <- waic(model4_bayes)
print(waic_model4)
```

```{r }
library(rstanarm)
library(tidyverse)
library(bayesplot)

# ==============================================================================
# 1. Basic Model Summary
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Model 4 Bayesian: Posterior Summary ===\n")
print(model4_bayes, digits = 3)

# ==============================================================================
# 2. Odds Ratios (more intuitive than log-odds)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Odds Ratios with 95% Credible Intervals ===\n")

# Extract posterior draws
posterior <- as.matrix(model4_bayes)

# Calculate odds ratios (exp of coefficients)
odds_ratios <- exp(posterior)

# Get posterior means and credible intervals
or_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = colnames(odds_ratios),
  OR = apply(odds_ratios, 2, mean),
  OR_Median = apply(odds_ratios, 2, median),
  CI_Lower = apply(odds_ratios, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025),
  CI_Upper = apply(odds_ratios, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975),
  Prob_OR_gt_1 = apply(odds_ratios, 2, function(x) mean(x > 1))
)

# Focus on key variables (exclude intercept and factor levels)
key_vars <- c("bmi_std", "grip_max", "pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", 
              "gh_nbs", "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs",
              "age", "female", "employed", "migback", "partner",
              "pgtatzeit", "pgbilzeit")

or_key <- or_summary %>%
  filter(Variable %in% key_vars) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(OR - 1)))

print(or_key, digits = 3)

cat("\nInterpretation of Odds Ratios:\n")
cat("OR > 1: Increases odds of attrition\n")
cat("OR < 1: Decreases odds of attrition\n")
cat("OR = 1: No effect\n")
cat("Prob_OR_gt_1: Probability that OR > 1 (Bayesian 'significance')\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 3. Percentage Change in Odds (most intuitive)
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Percentage Change in Odds of Attrition ===\n")

pct_change <- or_key %>%
  mutate(
    Pct_Change = (OR - 1) * 100,
    CI_Lower_Pct = (CI_Lower - 1) * 100,
    CI_Upper_Pct = (CI_Upper - 1) * 100,
    Direction = ifelse(Pct_Change > 0, "↑ Increases", "↓ Decreases")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Variable, Pct_Change, CI_Lower_Pct, CI_Upper_Pct, Direction, Prob_OR_gt_1)

print(pct_change, digits = 2)

cat("\nExample interpretation:\n")
cat("If BMI (std) has OR = 1.15, that's a 15% increase in odds of attrition\n")
cat("If pgtatzeit (years in study) has OR = 0.95, that's a 5% decrease per year\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 4. Education Variables - Interpretation
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Education Variables (Time in Study) ===\n")

edu_vars <- c("pgtatzeit", "pgbilzeit")
or_edu <- or_summary %>%
  filter(Variable %in% edu_vars)

print(or_edu, digits = 3)

cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
cat("pgtatzeit = Years respondent has been in SOEP\n")
cat("pgbilzeit = Years of education\n")
cat("\nEffect of 5 additional years:\n")

for(var in edu_vars) {
  or_5yr <- or_edu %>% filter(Variable == var) %>% pull(OR)
  or_5yr_effect <- or_5yr^5
  pct_5yr <- (or_5yr_effect - 1) * 100
  cat(sprintf("  %s: OR = %.3f → 5 years: OR = %.3f (%.1f%% change)\n",
              var, or_5yr, or_5yr_effect, pct_5yr))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 5. Interview Mode Effects
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Interview Mode Effects (factor(pgmode)) ===\n")

# Get all pgmode factor levels
pgmode_vars <- grep("factor\\(pgmode\\)", or_summary$Variable, value = TRUE)

if(length(pgmode_vars) > 0) {
  or_pgmode <- or_summary %>%
    filter(Variable %in% pgmode_vars) %>%
    mutate(
      Mode = gsub("factor\\(pgmode\\)", "", Variable),
      Pct_Change = (OR - 1) * 100
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(OR)) %>%
    dplyr::select(Mode, OR, CI_Lower, CI_Upper, Pct_Change, Prob_OR_gt_1)  # Add dplyr::
  
  print(or_pgmode, digits = 3)
  
  cat("\nReference category (OR = 1.0): Most common interview mode\n")
  cat("Interpretation: Compared to reference mode, how does each mode affect attrition?\n")
} else {
  cat("No factor(pgmode) variables found in model\n")
}

# ==============================================================================
# 6. Marginal Effects: Change in predicted probability
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Marginal Effects: Impact on Predicted Probability ===\n")

cat("\n1. SF-12 Health Subscales (10-point increase):\n")
cat("   Norm-based scores: mean=50, SD=10\n")
cat("   A 10-point change = 1 SD in population\n\n")

# Extract coefficients
coefs <- coef(model4_bayes)

# SF-12 variables
sf12_vars <- c("pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", "gh_nbs", 
               "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs")

marginal_sf12 <- data.frame(
  Variable = sf12_vars,
  Coef = coefs[sf12_vars],
  OR_per_10pts = exp(coefs[sf12_vars] * 10),
  Pct_Change_per_10pts = (exp(coefs[sf12_vars] * 10) - 1) * 100
)

print(marginal_sf12, digits = 3)

cat("\n2. Physical Health Variables:\n")
physical_vars <- c("bmi_std", "grip_max")
marginal_physical <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("BMI (1 SD increase)", "Grip strength (1 kg increase)"),
  Coef = coefs[physical_vars],
  OR = exp(coefs[physical_vars]),
  Pct_Change = (exp(coefs[physical_vars]) - 1) * 100
)
print(marginal_physical, digits = 3)

cat("\n3. Time Variables:\n")
time_vars <- c("age", "pgtatzeit", "pgbilzeit")
marginal_time <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Age (1 year)", "Years in SOEP (1 year)", "Years of education (1 year)"),
  Coef = coefs[time_vars],
  OR = exp(coefs[time_vars]),
  Pct_Change = (exp(coefs[time_vars]) - 1) * 100,
  OR_10yr = exp(coefs[time_vars] * 10),
  Pct_Change_10yr = (exp(coefs[time_vars] * 10) - 1) * 100
)
print(marginal_time, digits = 3)

# ==============================================================================
# 7. Visualizations
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Generating Visualizations ===\n")

# Get available parameters
available_pars <- names(coef(model4_bayes))

# Match key variables to actual parameter names
key_vars_actual <- available_pars[available_pars %in% key_vars]

cat("\nPlotting variables:\n")
print(key_vars_actual)

# Plot 1: Coefficient plot (log-odds scale)
p1 <- mcmc_intervals(model4_bayes, 
                     pars = key_vars_actual,
                     prob = 0.5, prob_outer = 0.95) +
  labs(title = "Posterior Distributions of Coefficients (Log-Odds)",
       subtitle = "50% (thick) and 95% (thin) credible intervals") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# Plot 2: Odds Ratios
or_plot_data <- or_key %>%
  filter(Variable %in% key_vars_actual)

p2 <- ggplot(or_plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Variable, OR), y = OR)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Odds Ratios with 95% Credible Intervals",
       subtitle = "OR > 1 increases attrition risk, OR < 1 decreases it",
       x = "", y = "Odds Ratio") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)

# Plot 3: Percentage change
pct_change_plot <- pct_change %>%
  filter(Variable %in% key_vars_actual)

p3 <- ggplot(pct_change_plot, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Pct_Change), y = Pct_Change)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, aes(color = Direction)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower_Pct, ymax = CI_Upper_Pct), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("↑ Increases" = "red", "↓ Decreases" = "blue")) +
  labs(title = "Percentage Change in Odds of Attrition",
       subtitle = "Effect of 1-unit increase in predictor",
       x = "", y = "% Change in Odds") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p3)

# Plot 4: Interview Mode Effects (if exists)
if(length(pgmode_vars) > 0) {
  mode_plot_data <- or_summary %>%
    filter(Variable %in% pgmode_vars) %>%
    mutate(Mode = gsub("factor\\(pgmode\\)", "", Variable))
  
  p4 <- ggplot(mode_plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Mode, OR), y = OR)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper), width = 0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = "Interview Mode Effects on Attrition",
         subtitle = "Odds ratios relative to reference mode",
         x = "Interview Mode", y = "Odds Ratio") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  print(p4)
}

# ==============================================================================
# 8. Key Findings Summary
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY ===\n\n")

# Identify strongest effects (main variables only)
strongest_increase <- or_key %>% 
  filter(Prob_OR_gt_1 > 0.95, OR > 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(OR)) %>%
  slice(1:5)

strongest_decrease <- or_key %>%
  filter(Prob_OR_gt_1 < 0.05, OR < 1) %>%
  arrange(OR) %>%
  slice(1:5)

cat("Factors that INCREASE attrition (>95% probability):\n")
if(nrow(strongest_increase) > 0) {
  for(i in 1:nrow(strongest_increase)) {
    cat(sprintf("  - %s: OR = %.3f (%.1f%% increase, 95%% CI: %.3f-%.3f)\n",
                strongest_increase$Variable[i],
                strongest_increase$OR[i],
                (strongest_increase$OR[i] - 1) * 100,
                strongest_increase$CI_Lower[i],
                strongest_increase$CI_Upper[i]))
  }
} else {
  cat("  None with >95% probability\n")
}

cat("\nFactors that DECREASE attrition (>95% probability):\n")
if(nrow(strongest_decrease) > 0) {
  for(i in 1:nrow(strongest_decrease)) {
    cat(sprintf("  - %s: OR = %.3f (%.1f%% decrease, 95%% CI: %.3f-%.3f)\n",
                strongest_decrease$Variable[i],
                strongest_decrease$OR[i],
                (1 - strongest_decrease$OR[i]) * 100,
                strongest_decrease$CI_Lower[i],
                strongest_decrease$CI_Upper[i]))
  }
} else {
  cat("  None with >95% probability\n")
}

# Special summary for education/time variables
cat("\n=== SUBSTANTIVE INTERPRETATION ===\n\n")

cat("Years in SOEP (pgtatzeit):\n")
pgt_or <- or_key %>% filter(Variable == "pgtatzeit") %>% pull(OR)
cat(sprintf("  Each additional year in study: %.1f%% change in attrition odds\n",
            (pgt_or - 1) * 100))
cat(sprintf("  10 years in study: %.1f%% cumulative change\n",
            (pgt_or^10 - 1) * 100))

cat("\nYears of Education (pgbilzeit):\n")
pgb_or <- or_key %>% filter(Variable == "pgbilzeit") %>% pull(OR)
cat(sprintf("  Each additional year of education: %.1f%% change in attrition odds\n",
            (pgb_or - 1) * 100))
cat(sprintf("  University degree vs high school (4 extra years): %.1f%% change\n",
            (pgb_or^4 - 1) * 100))



# ==============================================================================
# Focused Plot: Health + BMI + Grip + Age
# ==============================================================================

health_vars <- c(
  "pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", "gh_nbs",
  "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs",
  "bmi_std", "grip_max", "age"
)

plot_data <- or_summary %>%
  filter(Variable %in% health_vars) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = health_vars,
      labels = c(
        "Physical Functioning",
        "Role Physical",
        "Bodily Pain",
        "General Health",
        "Vitality",
        "Social Functioning",
        "Role Emotional",
        "Mental Health",
        "BMI (std)",
        "Grip Strength",
        "Age"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Variable, y = OR)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#E69F00") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = CI_Lower, ymax = CI_Upper),
    width = 0.15,
    color = "grey40"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Health, Physical, and Age Effects on Attrition",
    subtitle = "Odds ratios with 95% credible intervals",
    x = "",
    y = "Odds Ratio"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20")
  )

# ==============================================================================
# Split Plots: SF-12 Health vs Physical/Demographic Variables
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== Percentage Change in Odds: Split by Variable Type ===\n")

# SF-12 Health Subscales (8 variables)
sf12_vars <- c("pf_nbs", "rp_nbs", "bp_nbs", "gh_nbs",
               "vt_nbs", "sf_nbs", "re_nbs", "mh_nbs")

# Physical and demographic variables (use "femaleWomen" for the factor level)
other_vars <- c("bmi_std", "grip_max", "age", "femaleWomen", "employed", 
                "migback", "partner", "pgtatzeit", "pgbilzeit")

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 1: SF-12 Health Subscales Only (with meaningful interpretation)
# ==============================================================================

pct_sf12_data <- or_summary %>%
  filter(Variable %in% sf12_vars) %>%
  mutate(
    Pct_Change = (OR - 1) * 100,
    CI_Lower_Pct = (CI_Lower - 1) * 100,
    CI_Upper_Pct = (CI_Upper - 1) * 100,
    # Calculate effect for 10-point (1 SD) increase
    Pct_Change_10pt = (exp(log(OR) * 10) - 1) * 100,
    CI_Lower_10pt = (exp(log(CI_Lower) * 10) - 1) * 100,
    CI_Upper_10pt = (exp(log(CI_Upper) * 10) - 1) * 100,
    Variable_Label = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = sf12_vars,
      labels = c(
        "Physical Functioning",
        "Role Physical",
        "Bodily Pain",
        "General Health",
        "Vitality",
        "Social Functioning",
        "Role Emotional",
        "Mental Health"
      )
    )
  )

# Print SF-12 table with BOTH 1-point and 10-point effects
cat("\n--- SF-12 Health Subscales ---\n")
cat("Note: SF-12 scores are norm-based with mean=50, SD=10\n")
cat("      10-point change = 1 standard deviation in population\n\n")

sf12_table <- pct_sf12_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Variable_Label, 
         Pct_Change, CI_Lower_Pct, CI_Upper_Pct,
         Pct_Change_10pt, CI_Lower_10pt, CI_Upper_10pt,
         Prob_OR_gt_1) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Pct_Change_10pt)))

print(sf12_table, digits = 2)

cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
cat("- Pct_Change: Effect per 1-point increase (small change)\n")
cat("- Pct_Change_10pt: Effect per 10-point (1 SD) increase (meaningful change)\n")
cat("- Example: If Mental Health has -20% effect per 10 points:\n")
cat("    → Person scoring 60 (good) vs 40 (poor) has 20% LOWER attrition odds\n")
cat("    → Person scoring 70 (excellent) vs 50 (average) has 20% LOWER attrition odds\n\n")

# Create SF-12 plot with 10-POINT (1 SD) EFFECTS
p_sf12 <- ggplot(pct_sf12_data, aes(x = reorder(Variable_Label, Pct_Change_10pt), 
                                     y = Pct_Change_10pt)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5, color = "#0072B2") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = CI_Lower_10pt, ymax = CI_Upper_10pt),
    width = 0.2,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "SF-12 Health Subscales: Effect on Attrition",
    subtitle = "Percentage change in odds per 10-point (1 SD) increase\nin health score (95% CI)",
    x = "",
    y = "% Change in Odds of Attrition (per 10 points)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 11),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_sf12)

# ==============================================================================
# Additional interpretation: Clinical/practical examples
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== PRACTICAL INTERPRETATION OF SF-12 EFFECTS ===\n\n")

cat("SF-12 Score Reference Points:\n")
cat("  30-40 = Severe impairment (bottom 2%)\n")
cat("  40-50 = Below average health (bottom 16-50%)\n")
cat("  50    = Population average\n")
cat("  50-60 = Above average health (top 16-50%)\n")
cat("  60-70 = Excellent health (top 2-16%)\n\n")

# Show the most impactful SF-12 subscales
top_sf12 <- pct_sf12_data %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Pct_Change_10pt))) %>%
  dplyr::select(Variable_Label, Pct_Change_10pt, CI_Lower_10pt, CI_Upper_10pt) %>%
  head(3)

cat("Top 3 Most Impactful Health Dimensions:\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(top_sf12)) {
  direction <- ifelse(top_sf12$Pct_Change_10pt[i] > 0, "INCREASES", "DECREASES")
  cat(sprintf("\n%d. %s:\n", i, top_sf12$Variable_Label[i]))
  cat(sprintf("   → %.1f%% %s in attrition per 10-point improvement (95%% CI: %.1f to %.1f)\n",
              abs(top_sf12$Pct_Change_10pt[i]),
              direction,
              top_sf12$CI_Lower_10pt[i],
              top_sf12$CI_Upper_10pt[i]))
  cat(sprintf("   → Comparing someone at 40 (poor) vs 60 (good):\n"))
  cat(sprintf("      20-point difference = %.1f%% change in attrition odds\n",
              top_sf12$Pct_Change_10pt[i] * 2))
}

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT 2: Physical, Demographic, and Control Variables
# ==============================================================================

pct_other_data <- or_summary %>%
  filter(Variable %in% other_vars) %>%
  mutate(
    Pct_Change = (OR - 1) * 100,
    CI_Lower_Pct = (CI_Lower - 1) * 100,
    CI_Upper_Pct = (CI_Upper - 1) * 100,
    Variable_Label = factor(
      Variable,
      levels = other_vars,
      labels = c(
        "BMI (1 SD)",
        "Grip Strength (1 kg)",
        "Age (1 year)",
        "Female (vs Male)",
        "Employed",
        "Migration Background",
        "Has Partner",
        "Work Hours Per Week",
        "Years of Education"
      )
    )
  )

# Print other variables table
cat("\n\n--- Physical, Demographic, and Control Variables ---\n")
other_table <- pct_other_data %>%
  dplyr::select(Variable_Label, Pct_Change, CI_Lower_Pct, CI_Upper_Pct, Prob_OR_gt_1) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Pct_Change)))

print(other_table, digits = 2)

cat("\nNote: Effects per unit increase (see labels for units)\n")
cat("Binary variables (Female, Employed, Migration Background, Has Partner):\n")
cat("  → Shows % change comparing 1 (yes) vs 0 (no)\n\n")

# Create other variables plot
p_other <- ggplot(pct_other_data, aes(x = reorder(Variable_Label, Pct_Change), y = Pct_Change)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5, color = "#D55E00") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = CI_Lower_Pct, ymax = CI_Upper_Pct),
    width = 0.2,
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Physical, Demographic, and Control Variables:\nEffect on Attrition",
    subtitle = "Percentage change in odds per unit increase (95% CI)",
    x = "",
    y = "% Change in Odds of Attrition"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 11),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_other)

# ==============================================================================
# Summary Statistics
# ==============================================================================

cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n\n")

cat("SF-12 Health Subscales:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean absolute effect: %.2f%%\n", mean(abs(pct_sf12_data$Pct_Change_10pt))))
cat(sprintf("  Range: %.2f%% to %.2f%%\n", 
            min(pct_sf12_data$Pct_Change_10pt), 
            max(pct_sf12_data$Pct_Change_10pt)))

cat("\nPhysical/Demographic Variables:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mean absolute effect: %.2f%%\n", mean(abs(pct_other_data$Pct_Change))))
cat(sprintf("  Range: %.2f%% to %.2f%%\n", 
            min(pct_other_data$Pct_Change), 
            max(pct_other_data$Pct_Change)))

```



# ==============================================================================
# Robustness check, Right Censoring 
# ==============================================================================

```{r }

library(rstanarm)
library(bayesplot)


d_analysis_robust <- d_analysis %>%
  filter(syear < 2020)   # Exclude 2020 
  

# ==============================================================================
# Model 4 Bayesian: Full model with socioeconomic controls
# ==============================================================================

set.seed(42)  # For reproducibility

# Fit Bayesian logistic regression with weakly informative priors
model4_bayes_robust <- stan_glm(
  attrition ~ bmi_std + grip_max +
    pf_nbs + rp_nbs + bp_nbs + gh_nbs + vt_nbs + sf_nbs + re_nbs + mh_nbs +
    age + female + employed + migback + partner + 
    pgtatzeit + pgbilzeit + factor(pgmode) +
    factor(psample) + factor(syear),
  data = d_analysis_robust,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  
  # Priors
  prior = normal(0, 2.5),           # Weakly informative prior for coefficients
  prior_intercept = normal(0, 5),   # Prior for intercept
  
  # MCMC settings
  chains = 4,                        # Number of Markov chains
  iter = 2000,                       # Total iterations per chain
  warmup = 1000,                     # Burn-in iterations
  cores = 8,                         # Parallel processing
  refresh = 250,                     # Progress updates
  
  # Diagnostics
  seed = 42
)

cat("\n=== Model 4 Bayesian: Full Model with Controls ===\n")
print(model4_bayes_robust, digits = 3)

# Model diagnostics
cat("\n=== Bayesian Diagnostics ===\n")
cat("R-hat values (should be < 1.01):\n")
rhat_vals <- summary(model4_bayes_robust)[, "Rhat"]
print(rhat_vals)
cat("\nAny problematic R-hat values?:", any(rhat_vals > 1.01, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

cat("\nEffective sample sizes (should be > 400):\n")
neff_vals <- summary(model4_bayes_robust)[, "n_eff"]
print(summary(neff_vals))
cat("Any low ESS?:", any(neff_vals < 400, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Posterior predictive checks
cat("\n=== Posterior Predictive Checks ===\n")

# Check mean attrition rate
pp_check(model4_bayes_robust, plotfun = "stat", stat = "mean") +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Mean Attrition Rate",
       subtitle = "Does the model reproduce the observed attrition rate?")

# Check binary outcome distribution
pp_check(model4_bayes_robust, plotfun = "bars", nreps = 50) +
  labs(title = "Posterior Predictive Check: Binary Outcome Distribution",
       subtitle = "Observed (dark) vs Predicted (light) attrition")

# Compare with first model
cat("\n=== Comparison: Frequentist vs Bayesian ===\n")
# Get coefficients from both models
freq_coefs <- coef(model4_bayes)
bayes_coefs <- coef(model4_bayes_robust)


# Find common variable names
common_vars <- intersect(names(freq_coefs), names(bayes_coefs))

# Create comparison dataframe with only common variables
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = common_vars,
  Frequentist = freq_coefs[common_vars],
  Bayesian = bayes_coefs[common_vars],
  Difference = bayes_coefs[common_vars] - freq_coefs[common_vars]
)

# View the comparison
print(comparison_df, digits = 3)

# Credible intervals (Bayesian) vs Confidence intervals (Frequentist)
cat("\n=== 95% Credible Intervals (Bayesian Robust) ===\n")
bayes_ci <- posterior_interval(model4_bayes_robust, prob = 0.95)
print(head(bayes_ci, 15))  # Show first 15 for readability

cat("\n=== 95% Confidence Intervals (First Bayesian) ===\n")
freq_ci <- posterior_interval(model4_bayes, prob = 0.95)
print(head(freq_ci, 15))



# Model comparison metrics
cat("\n=== Model Fit Metrics ===\n")
cat("LOO (Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation):\n")
loo_model4 <- loo(model4_bayes_robust)
print(loo_model4)

cat("\nWAIC (Watanabe-Akaike Information Criterion):\n")
waic_model4 <- waic(model4_bayes_robust)
print(waic_model4)
```
